element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Combat fundamentals against humanoid infected and early alien creatures
Distinguish threats from environmental hazards; manage ammo conservation
Learn unpredictable movement patterns; develop reactive combat skills

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Mid-game (merchant freighter or command building area)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Area-denial tactics; crowd control against dense enemy clusters
Spatial awareness; navigation around damaged sections; time pressure awareness
Directional orientation in 3D space; understanding level hierarchy
Password progression gating; incentive structure for completionism

typical_relief_triggers: Clearing all enemies from a chamber
typical_tension_triggers: Time pressure from cooling system overload
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Ambush-driven tension with labyrinthine exploration creating sustained uncertainty; reviewed as causing 'severe anxiety' from unpredictable threats

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Thorough enemy elimination unlocks passwords; player can skip to exit without completion

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to basic combat and alien creature behavior on Europa-1 warship; foundational weapon familiarity
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Abandoned merchant freighter introduces new environment types and denser enemy clustering; flamethrower acquisition enables tactical variety
difficulty_curve.late_game: Planet Defense Corps command building and sub-basement areas; intensified alien presence and environmental complexity
notable_spikes: Transition from human-dominated to alien-dominated enemy mix
Introduction of flamethrower encounters (enemy and player possession)
Sub-basement levels (increased spatial complexity and threat density)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level presents an elevator or staircase exit that must be reached to progress. Players can bypass enemy elimination, but password progression is contingent on thorough clearance, creating optional challenge framing
Levels feature large, interconnected chamber networks with multiple approach routes and dead-ends. Reviewed as 'labyrinthine,' contributing to sustained exploration tension and replayability
Damaged warship sections introduce fire and cooling system threats as spatial obstacles. Reinforces narrative (damaged flagship) while complicating movement and sightlines
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Europa-1 sections with fire damage noted in briefing
